summary: Implement fixed GameDay scheduler and deterministic system order
acceptance_criteria: Authority advances in whole fixed GameDay steps; UI speed, render framerate, wall-clock time, locale, and timezone cannot affect committed state.
System execution order is explicit, versioned, exported for tests, and locked by a golden/order test with stable tie-breakers.
Day, month, year, and 3650-day boundary behavior is covered without using real Date objects in authority logic.
Scheduler state is part of WorldState v0 or an explicit child state and participates in deterministic hash inputs where relevant.
A 3650-day headless run over the minimal M1 state completes with no invariant violation.
A static guard rejects Date.now, performance.now, new Date, setTimeout/setInterval scheduling, or render-frame inputs in authoritative sim-core logic.
A baseline performance command records day throughput and step timing distribution for the minimal M1 fixture; no optimization-specific abstraction is added without measured need.
required_tests: Golden/order tests cover fixed system order and stable tie-breakers.
Invariant/property tests cover 3650-day advancement and calendar boundaries.
Performance baseline output records fixture, machine/runtime context where available, day throughput, and P50/P95/P99 step timing.
notes: Keep scheduler minimal for M1; monthly/yearly hooks may be empty, but their order and determinism contract must be explicit.
Do not introduce real gameplay systems while creating scheduler phases.
